hypothesis: If LLM agents guided by a ReACT workflow can autonomously infer the governing rules and optimal operating conditions of a simulated ALP reactor using only pressure and QCM data within a fixed experimental budget, then their inferred rules will align with expert-labeled references and yield high UMAP concordance and manual evaluation scores, because the reactor dynamics (Arrhenius kinetics, temperature-dependent decomposition, and coupled transport and surface reactions) encode observable outcomes the agents must learn.
